text: Every year, American industrial businesses spend over $2.9 trillion sourcing the materials,
equipment, and parts that keep our factories running.
Globally, that number climbs past $9 trillion.
But what happens when the people who know how to run America's factories leave?
We believe there's always room for improvement, so we're always trying to upgrade things and improve things.
They can't afford to get the wrong thing or wait too long.
They need someone who knows exactly what fits their machines and get it to them fast.
Factories run on tight schedules.
If a critical part fails, every minute counts.
Getting the right part fast is the difference between a quick fix and a costly downtime.
I come, check my diary, see who visited yesterday, what they need, and get their quotes ready.
I push through as many as I can before heading out to see customers.
Then come back and try to finish the rest.
It's a balancing act between staying on top of what's pending and tackling what's just come in.
In industrial America, a silent crisis is brewing.
The software powering this industry is decades old and with over 2 million unfulfilled jobs by 2030 and half the sales knowledge set to retire, the cracks are showing.
We can't build the future on top of a broken foundation.
That's why we built Avid, the future of industrial commerce.
An AI agent that sits inside the tools you already use.
It tracks incoming requests from email, fax, and phone.
When a customer sends in an RFQ, the system finds the right pricing, generates a code, and sends it out.
When a purchase order comes in, it's matched against the code that sales order is created and entered into the ERP.
A human still reviews everything before it goes out.
With Avid, your team can focus on what truly matters, building strong customer relationships.
